blocks:
Sūrat al-Tīn (The Fig)

Meccan, and it is eight verses.

Sūrat al-Tīn (The Fig)

Meccan, and it is eight verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) وَالتِّينِ وَالزَّيْتُونِ ** (1) (By the fig and the olive) It was said that He singled them out from the fruits for the oath because the fig is a good fruit that has no waste, and a subtle nourishment quick to digest, and a medicine of great benefit, for it softens the nature, dissolves the phlegm (البلغم), purifies the kidneys, removes the sand (رمل المثانة) of the bladder, opens the obstruction (سدّة) of the liver and the spleen, and nourishes the body. And in the Hadith: that it cures hemorrhoids (البواسير) and benefits against gout (النقرس). And the olive is a fruit, a condiment (إدام), and a medicine, and it has a subtle oil (دهن لطيف) of many benefits.

(٢) وَطُورِ سِينِينَ ** (2) And Mount Sinai, It is said that He means by it the mountain upon which Moses supplicated his Lord, and سِينِينَ ** and سَيْناءَ ** are two names for the place where it is located. (٣) وَهذَا الْبَلَدِ الْأَمِينِ ** (3) and this secure city! meaning the secure (الآمن), which is Mecca (مكّة).

In al-Khisal and al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, chose four cities, and He, the Almighty, said: وَالتِّينِ وَالزَّيْتُونِ وَطُورِ سِينِينَ وَهذَا الْبَلَدِ الْأَمِينِ ** (1) By the fig, (2) and the olive, (3) and Mount Sinai, (4) and this secure city! So, the Fig (التّين) is Medina (المدينة), the Olive (الزيتون) is the Holy House (البيت المقدّس), Mount Sinai (طور سينين) is Kufa (الكوفة), and this Secure City (هذا البلد الأمين) is Mecca (مكّة)."

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: التِّينِ ** is the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, الزَّيْتُونِ ** is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), طُورِ سِينِينَ ** is al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them both), and وَهذَا الْبَلَدِ الْأَمِينِ ** is the Imams (peace be upon them).

And in al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him): التِّينِ وَالزَّيْتُونِ ** are al-Hasan and al-Husayn (peace be upon them both), Mount Sinai (طُورِ سَيْناءَ) is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), and وَهذَا الْبَلَدِ الْأَمِينِ** is Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم.

(4) لَقَدْ خَلَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ فِي أَحْسَنِ تَقْوِيمٍ ** (4) We have certainly created man in the best of moulds. By singling him out with the uprightness of stature, the beauty of form, the comprehensiveness of the properties (خواصّ) of the beings (الكائنات), and the counterparts (نظائر) of the rest of the existing things (الموجودات). (5) ثُمَّ رَدَدْناهُ أَسْفَلَ سافِلِينَ ** (5) Then We relegated him to the lowest of the low, It was said that We made him among the inhabitants of the Fire (النّار). Al-Qummi (said) it was revealed concerning the first (man/creation). And in al-Manaqib, narrated from al-Kazim (peace be upon him), he said: The man (الْإِنْسانَ) is the first (man/creation). **ثُمَّ رَدَدْناهُ أَسْفَلَ سافِلِينَ ** (Then We relegated him to the lowest of the low) due to his hatred of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(6) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ فَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ ** (except those who have faith and do righteous deeds, there is an unfailing reward for them.) He said: Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام. (7) فَما يُكَذِّبُكَ بَعْدُ ** (What makes you deny after this?) So what thing makes you deny, O Muhammad, by way of indication (دلالة) or utterance (نطقاً), after the appearance of these signs (الدلائل)? This is how it was said regarding بِالدِّينِ. In Hadith al-Manaqib (حديث المناقب): [It refers] to the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib عليه السلام. And it was said: [It refers] to the recompense (الجزاء). And al-Qummi [said regarding] إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (except those who have faith): He said: That is the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. [Regarding] بِالدِّينِ (by the religion/judgment): He said: By the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام. ** فَلَهُمْ أَجْرٌ غَيْرُ مَمْنُونٍ ** (there is an unfailing reward for them) meaning: He will not remind them of His favor concerning it (لا يمنّ عليهم به).

**(٨) أَلَيْسَ اللهُ بِأَحْكَمِ الْحاكِمِينَ ** (8) Is not Allah the most just of judges?

A confirmation of what preceded. Meaning: Is not the One Who did that—the creation and the return (الردّ)—the most just of judges (بِأَحْكَمِ الْحاكِمِينَ) in making (صنعاً) and planning (وتدبيراً)? And whoever is like that is capable of the return (resurrection) and the recompense.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله, and in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida عليه السلام, that both of them said upon finishing it: "Yes (بلى), and I am among those who bear witness to that (وانَا عَلى ذلكَ من الشاهِدين)."

And in al-Khisal: The like of it is narrated from the Commander of the Faithful عليه السلام, concerning what He taught His companions.

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Whoever recites Wa al-Tin (والتّين) in his obligatory (فرائضه) and supererogatory (ونوافله) prayers will be given from Paradise (الجنّة) wherever he is pleased.
